name: Beef n' Bean Burritos
recipeInstructions:
Serves 4 to 6
Lightly grease Crock-Pot. Prepare foil handles by cutting three 20 x 3 inch strips of heavy-duty foil or use regular foil folded to double thickness. Place the handles in the Crock-Pot.
In a saucepan, combine enchilada mix, water, tomato paste, black pepper, garlic powder, and salt. Simmer for 15 minutes over low heat. In a skillet, brown ground beef. Drain excess fat. Stir one-third of the sauce into the browned beef.
Spread a thin layer of refried beans on each flour tortilla. Place the tortillas on top of the sauce in the Crock-Pot, tearing them to fit if necessary. Spoon meat mixture over each tortilla and then top with a small amount of cheese. Continue layering until the top of the Crock-Pot is reached, ending with a layer of cheese.
Cover and cook on Low for 6 to 8 hours or on High for 3 to 4 hours. Remove burritos from the Crock-Pot by the foil handles and place on a serving plate. Cut into wedges and garnish as desired with taco sauce, sour cream, salsa, chopped onion, chili peppers, or guacamole.
